coin_profile.name: NEAR Protocol
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: NEAR Protocol is a developer-friendly Layer 1 blockchain with a focus on usability and scalability. It uses a sharding technique called Nightshade to achieve high throughput. NEAR is notable for its human-readable account names and low transaction costs.

NEAR.name: NEAR Protocol

moat.rationale: NEAR has moderate moat strength anchored by solid network demand (10) and holder value accrual (10), but faces competitive pressures typical of Layer 1s. While it offers genuine sharding technology and developer-friendly tooling with growing ecosystem lock-in, it remains forked-able, lacks ETH's settlement finality or SOL's speed advantages, and competes in a crowded L1 space where users/capital can migrate to cheaper alternatives. The protocol's youth (6 maturity) and moderate fee growth (6) indicate it hasn't yet achieved Bitcoin/Ethereum-grade defensibility, though its tokenomics and network fundamentals suggest potential for moat deepening.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.

forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Fee-based methods (Fee/MC Multiple) used DeFiLlama protocol fee data to anchor the valuation. Fee/MC Multiple produced a value below 10% of current price (fees are low relative to market cap) — shown in the breakdown chart but excluded from the median pool. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

bull_points: <cite index="5-22">Arthur Hayes named NEAR a top altcoin on May 23, 2026, triggering a 50% weekly surge and heavy short liquidations</cite>, creating near-term momentum and potential for further rally as institutional attention increases.

<cite index="5-3,5-13">A Bitwise spot NEAR ETF filed in May 2025 is pending SEC approval expected around September 2026, which would open the door for regulated institutional capital inflows</cite> and significantly improve liquidity.

<cite index="2-21">NEAR dropped to about $1.25 on May 4 and then quickly recovered to $2.8 by May 26, 2026</cite>, establishing strong support and recovery momentum that could drive a mean reversion bounce over the next 3-6 months.
bear_points: <cite index="1-14">The RSI value is at 87.47, indicating that the NEAR market is in an overbought position</cite>, increasing the probability of a sharp pullback before the next leg higher.

<cite index="2-28,2-29,2-30">Technical data from late May 2026 shows a mixed-to-bearish outlook on the long-term chart, with Technical Indicators carrying an overall Sell rating despite short-term support, suggesting NEAR still faces broader downside pressure</cite>.

<cite index="2-7,2-8,2-9">2025 proved to be a challenging year for NEAR, with the asset experiencing a sharp, sustained decline throughout the first quarter, drifting down toward the $1.5 level by late December</cite>, reflecting sustained weakness that may persist.

bull_points: <cite index="5-5">NEAR led all major blockchains with over 950 GitHub commits in H1 2025</cite>, demonstrating sustained high development activity and a quality score of 7.6/10 (Safe), positioning it among the better-quality protocols for long-term value creation.

<cite index="6-19,6-20">Daily transactions on the NEAR Protocol chain frequently surpass 8 million with peak transaction processing at 4,135 TPS, suggesting strong and sustainable growth rather than a short-lived spike in interest</cite>.

<cite index="5-3,5-8">NEAR's post-quantum cryptography integration with FIPS-204 standards positions the protocol as a leader in blockchain security by safeguarding assets against future quantum computing threats</cite>, creating long-term competitive advantages.
bear_points: <cite index="6-28">The market for Layer-1 blockchains is highly competitive</cite>, and with NEAR's MC/Fees ratio of 936x, the protocol needs substantial fee growth and adoption acceleration over 5 years to justify current valuations.

<cite index="5-15">The key risk is regulatory rejection of pending ETF applications, which could lead to negative sentiment and selling pressure</cite>, particularly if approval is not granted by the September 2026 deadline.

<cite index="3-14">Despite NEAR advancing its technology and forging strategic partnerships, questions surrounding its current price potential persist</cite>, reflecting execution risk and uncertainty about whether technical upgrades will translate into meaningful developer adoption and revenue acceleration.
